conflict: The school disintegrating — DA in open revolt, Carrows losing control, Harry Potter rumors — while Gavin tries to work at the edge of comprehension on ward-maintenance problems designed for a non-human intelligence
consequence: The human war becomes unavoidable. Gavin's reflexive, instinctive substrate edit when healing a first-year's wrist shows that the First Art is becoming automatic, not just deliberate. The night before the Battle, the substrate is flexing with the approaching conflict
must_land_beats: The school in chaos: DA defiance, Carrow violence, students hiding in the Room of Requirement, the atmosphere of imminent rupture

Gavin working at the outer edge of comprehension — the ward-maintenance problems are designed for a civilization he cannot match, and the gap between what is needed and what he can do is the real pressure

The instinctive substrate edit: healing a first-year's broken wrist in a way that is subtly wrong — not magical healing but a substrate correction. Gavin catches it and reverts to conventional healing before anyone notices
The eve of battle: Gavin in the sub-collection, the substrate flexing with approaching conflict, noting the perturbation the way you note weather while working
pressure_source: The school disintegrating — DA in open revolt, Carrows losing control, Harry Potter rumors — while Gavin tries to work at the edge of comprehension on ward-maintenance problems designed for a non-human intelligence
state_shift: The human war becomes unavoidable. Gavin's reflexive, instinctive substrate edit when healing a first-year's wrist shows that the First Art is becoming automatic, not just deliberate. The night before the Battle, the substrate is flexing with the approaching conflict
